reasoning_chain_text: 1. Identify Washington counties with low-income enrollment percentage between 32% and 40% in 2018-19.
2. Keep counties meeting that same range in 2019-20.
3. Keep counties meeting that same range in 2020-21.
4. For 2021-22, intersect the four yearly low-income sets to keep counties meeting the range every year.
5. From those counties, keep the ones with district-average math proficiency above 40% in 2021-22.
6. Determine whether the county in dataset position 6 is the most populous county in Washington.
7. Record the 2020 population for the county in dataset position 7 if it remains under consideration after step 6.
8. Record the 2020 population and county seat for the county in dataset position 8, then identify which later county has the lower population among the counties still under consideration.
9. Identify the town that beat that county seat in the agricultural college site-selection competition.
10. Record the date when the agricultural college in dataset position 10 first opened to students.
original_final_question: On what date did the agricultural college first open to students, given that this college was ultimately located in a town that beat the county seat (in a site selection competition) of the Washington county that had the lowest 2020 census population among those Washington counties that (1) were NOT the most populous in the state, (2) had district-average Math proficiency above 40% in the 2021-22 school year, AND (3) had Low-Income student enrollment percentage between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22? Report in the format MM/DD/YYYY. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=4, Washington Report Card Enrollment 2018-22 - nodes 1-4):
Node 1: 2018-19 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Whitman}
Node 2: 2019-20 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Whitman}
Node 3: 2020-21 Low-Income 32-40% -> {Island, King, Kitsap, San Juan, Snohomish, Thurston, Whitman}
Node 4: 2021-22 Low-Income 32-40% -> {King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman}
Intersection: {King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman}
HOP 2 (d=2, Assessment data + King Wikipedia - nodes 5-6):
Node 5 (Assessment 2021-22): Math proficiency >40% -> {King, Kitsap, Whitman} (eliminates San Juan at 35.2%)
Node 6 (King Wikipedia): King is most populous -> NOT most populous = {Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman}
Intersection: {Kitsap, Whitman}
HOP 3 (d=2, Wikipedia county articles - nodes 7-8):
Node 7 (Kitsap Wikipedia): 2020 population = 275,611
Node 8 (Whitman Wikipedia): 2020 population = 47,973; county seat is Colfax
Comparison: 47,973 < 275,611 -> Whitman has lower population
Result: Whitman County, county seat = Colfax
HOP 4 (d=1, Colfax Wikipedia - node 9):
Node 9: Colfax competed for WSU location in 1889-90, lost to Pullman
Result: Washington State University
HOP 5 (d=1, WSU Wikipedia - node 10):
Node 10: WSU opened to students on January 13, 1892
Result: January 13, 1892
Final answer: January 13, 1892
